verdict.answer_first: A and B both lead with outcome: A "It's an infinite loop"; B "It's an infinite loop".
verdict.decisiveness: A wins: it says "integer-counter version is better"; B dilutes with "Fixes, roughly in order of preference" and 3 options.
verdict.fabrication: A invents/overstates "until x overflows to Infinity"; B gives an unverified approximate "around 2^53" but labels it "around".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on: one preferred prescription, "for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) { tick(i * 0.1); }" plus why.
